facts.claim: Dunedin is the second-most populous city in the South Island of New Zealand, and the principal city of the Otago region.

Dunedin (/dʌˈniːdɪn/ ⓘ dun-EE-din;[10][a] Māori: Ōtepoti) is the second-most populous city in the South Island of New Zealand (after Christchurch), and the principal city of the Otago region. Its name comes from Dùn Èideann ("fort of Edin"), the Scottish Gaelic name for Edinburgh, the capital of Scotland.[12]
